description: Fix plugin search display issues: fetchPackageInfo incorrectly parses npm maintainer strings as objects (they are strings like 'name <email>'), causing all direct-lookup plugins to show 'by unknown'. Known minor issues NOT addressed: empty plugin description is a package metadata issue (separate PRD).

userStories.title: Fix fetchPackageInfo maintainer string parsing
userStories.description: As a user running `opentabs plugin search`, I want to see the correct author name for plugins found via direct registry lookup so that I can identify who maintains the plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: fetchPackageInfo correctly extracts the author name from npm view --json maintainers array, where entries are strings like 'name <email>'
The parsed author name (before the '<') is used as the publisher username
If the maintainer string has no angle brackets, the entire string is used as the name
If the maintainers array is empty or parsing fails, publisher falls back to undefined (shows 'unknown')
Unit tests cover: string maintainer format 'name <email>', string without email, empty array, and object format (backwards compatibility)
